content: I just finished filming my latest YouTube video - it's all about making the perfect croissant. Took me three attempts to get the lamination right.
That's impressive dedication! What was the biggest challenge you faced with the lamination process?
Honestly? The butter kept melting in my kitchen. It's been so hot in San Francisco lately - my bees are struggling too.
Oh, you keep bees? That's fascinating. Does it connect to your pastry work at all?
Absolutely! I use my own honey in some of my signature pastries at La Belle Époque. The lavender honey is incredible in madeleines.
That sounds amazing. I'd love to try those someday. How long have you been working at La Belle Époque?
Almost five years now. I started as a line cook and worked my way up. The head chef there is brutal but taught me everything.
What's the most valuable lesson you learned from them?
Precision. Every gram matters. I've been applying that to my Rust programming projects too - there's something satisfying about precise code.
That's a great crossover skill. Do you find baking and coding have similar problem-solving approaches?
Definitely. Both require troubleshooting when things don't turn out right. Like when my sourdough starter died last month - had to completely rebuild it from scratch, just like debugging a program.
